name: Jay Inslee
subject: My message from the State of the State:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
It's the time to come together. We all have a stake in this fight. | [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79cb5/2f5ea88d/73d8a619/2cbc9e08/296701880/VEsH/ "https://www.facebook.com/jayinslee/posts/10156059206423466") Friend, We should be proud of the progress we've made here in Washington. But we're sure as heck not stopping now. We know we can do big things, because we've done them before. Look, pundits said we'd never be able to pass a transportation package. But now, we have the greenest transportation package in state history. Insiders doubted we would be able to make huge investments in funding our schools. But we did -- and now our children and grandchildren have all-day kindergarten, smaller class sizes, and more opportunity to start achieving early. They said we'd hurt business by raising the minimum wage, providing paid sick leave, and implementing the nation's best paid family leave policy. Yet we were named the best state for business in the nation. That's why during my State of the State Address earlier this week, we committed to even bigger fights ahead -- for our economy, for our schools, for our health, and for our climate. [**Share this post about the speech with your friends and family on Facebook to spread the message: We're getting to work.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79cb5/2f5ea88d/73d8a619/2cbc9e08/296701880/VEsE/ "https://www.facebook.com/jayinslee/posts/10156059206423466") During my speech, I reiterated a commitment that you and I know all too well: We must recognize that there is an existential threat to the health of our state, our children, and our jobs. It's time to act, before the threat becomes our new reality. That threat is climate change. If anyone can lead the country in the global effort to reduce greenhouse gas emissions, it's Washington state. We are creators, inventors, builders. Together, we can build the carbon-free future our grandchildren deserve. Washington is exactly the right state to lead the clean-energy economy. And that's why it is time to join our West Coast neighbors and institute a price on carbon. The global economy is moving towards a decarbonized, clean-energy future. Countries like China and India are acting. We must not fall behind in this race for clean energy. And by doing our part to take action right here in Washington state, we can confront a clear and present danger. Change can be daunting, but America's innovative spirit has overcome challenges before. The space race not only took man to the moon, but it spawned advances in science, medicine, and technology that catapulted our economy into the information age. We are at the cusp of a similar opportunity. We will either stand up to save our planet and build the vibrant, innovative, and sustainable economy of the future; or we will shrink from the challenge, allow others to take the lead, and lose our ability to protect our communities and define our own fates. Now is the time to come together, Democrats and Republicans, business and labor leaders, rural Washington and urban Washington, young and old, because we all have a stake in this fight. Let's do this. [**Thanks for sharing this post about an important speech.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79cb5/2f5ea88d/73d8a619/2cbc9e08/296701880/VEsF/ "https://www.facebook.com/jayinslee/posts/10156059206423466") Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [SHARE »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79cb5/2f5ea88d/73d8a619/2cbc9e08/296701880/VEsC/ "https://www.facebook.com/jayinslee/posts/10156059206423466")

subject: Sign now: Support common sense gun laws that save lives.
body:
**Support Common Sense Gun Laws That Save Lives** ** ** **In November, 26 people were shot and killed at the First Baptist Church in Sutherland Springs, Texas, and dozens more were hospitalized. And almost on cue, NRA-backed politicians started parroting the claim that there 's nothing to be done to end the gun violence that kills 93 Americans every day in our country.**
**[If you think this is unacceptable, sign the petition and join Everytown For Gun Safety.](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/W5lXJ/6/xb/r5cs/6Rq6y0/6n1hmXSpjX/P/P)** **[](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/W5lXJ/s/xb/r5cs/6Rq6y0/sjlT0QnQux/P/P)**** ** **For too long, the NRA has been pushing their dangerous "guns everywhere" agenda -- meaning that too often, profits have been prioritized over the safety of the American people. And we've had enough.** ** ** **The horrific shootings in Las Vegas and Sutherland Springs force us as a nation to decide: Will we accept the status quo and allow mass shootings and gun homicides to be the backdrop to our daily lives? Or do we want to live in a country where we can go to the movies or a concert or school or work or church and be safe from gun violence?** ** ** **The time to act is now. While our country continues to mourn, NRA leadership is still trying to loosen our gun safety laws and grow the gun industry 's profit margin at any cost. We can't let them, and we can't wait for another mass shooting before we take action. [Join us today!](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/W5lXJ/v/xb/r5cs/6Rq6y0/6fwm9aAE43/P/P)** **Together, we will keep fighting to pass common sense gun laws that save lives.** **Thank you for your support,** **Everytown for Gun Safety**

name: The White House
subject: King’s dream is the American dream
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=ced31f3d2e8882e38579efd6e80e84dbac506265661519c769467b606167d143be929e2b430b596193eee4db8d64e0e718a9f05ad7d34de0

King’s dream is the American dream

Today, President Donald J. Trump will sign the official proclamation making this Monday, January 15, the “Martin Luther King Jr. Federal Holiday.” This year marks the first time in more than a decade that the federal holiday falls on King’s actual birthday.

Here is an advance look at what the President will tell Americans today:

- Reverend King preached love throughout his life—love for each other, for our fellow Americans, and for humanity. That is what drove his work.
- We celebrate King first and foremost for standing up for the self-evident truth Americans hold so dear: No matter the color of our skin, or the place of our birth, we are ALL created equal by God.
- This April will mark half a century since King was cruelly taken from us by an assassin’s bullet. As we mourn his loss, we also pledge to fight for his dream of equality, freedom, justice, and peace.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=ced31f3d2e8882e35c36fb8825f8c7c06ed182ea7df756a25e35b52e76b6dedc9914074db703b6335808af5761d459788df5af057b9f5f1f
Watch President Trump sign the proclamation live at 11:30 a.m. ET today.

‘We need to do more than simply remove people’

“America has 5 percent of the world’s population, but we have 25 percent of the world’s incarcerated,” Texas Public Policy Foundation President Brooke Rollins told reporters before a meeting with President Trump on prison reform yesterday.

The White House meeting comes as part of the Administration’s effort to improve the U.S. prison system. It brought together prison reform experts—including two governors who are leading the charge at the state level.

Governors Matt Bevin (R-KY) and Sam Brownback (R-KS) walked through how their states have implemented changes in the correctional system, focusing on how to help prisoners become productive contributors to society upon release.

“We’ll be very tough on crime, but we will provide a ladder of opportunity to the future,” President Trump said.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=ced31f3d2e8882e35e9bbce4aca9b6c44b631e1e609d4e30a4107ee4107ea344981abb7836dbe8863e3a7acdb42590a250937d57b6b4b56c
Read more: “We need to do more than simply remove people from society.”

‘More dangerous than they’ve ever been’

Earlier this week, President Trump signed the INTERDICT Act, a bipartisan effort to provide Customs and Border Protection officers with the equipment needed to detect fentanyl—“our new big [drug] scourge,” the President said.

“In 2016, nearly 20,000 Americans died as a result of using synthetic opioids such as fentanyl,” he continued. “The drugs, for a lot of reasons, are far more dangerous than they’ve ever been.”

“It’s something that is absolutely urgent. It’s the number one killer now, in terms of overdoses, in . . . Massachusetts,” Sen. Rob Portman (R-OH) said. “And, unfortunately, over the past several years, it has increased every single year.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=ced31f3d2e8882e33ab3a36decf467190c26bc3c949e24826917cac5aa8907a6dbe5ad985a7ac22edbd45558d3fe86aeb2eecd3769192388
Watch: President Trump Signs INTERDICT Act to Improve Opioid Screening Technology.

Photo of the Day

United States Secretary of the Treasury Steven Mnuchin | January 11, 2018 (Official White House Photo by Andrea Hanks)

POTUS TODAY

This morning, President Trump will sign a proclamation to honor Dr. Martin Luther, King Jr. Day. Later, the President will receive his annual physical exam at Walter Reed National Military Medical Center.

Vice President Mike Pence will participate in a phone call with Prime Minister Mateusz Morawiecki of Poland.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=ced31f3d2e8882e35e87b192af08d439753ce483e18bc00413ad12d9c804717ea243bf277ad25231573146d6d1e23ae255ecbbdef6b18724

Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d4a57f0241f68c065e5992bd0300314e73a3c414266b2abdfdb0d11f8a8776a90f876be605df1dd8fe17f19703e1ffc926

subject: I have an important request, Derek
body:
You can make a difference in this election. Become a monthly sustaining donor today:
---
Derek --

**[The best thing you can do right now to help take back Congress this year is become a monthly sustaining contributor to the Democratic Party.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522313/4067b0a/4aed063eb/7e5ea29d/1967342944/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dnc_monthly_sustainer?recurring=true&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+The+best+thing+you+can+do+right+now+to+h&utm_campaign=em_20180112_lc_nd&source=em_20180112_lc_nd&refcode=em_20180112_lc_nd")**

This year, Democrats have a real chance at taking back the House and flipping state chambers all over the country. We even have a chance to regain a majority in the Senate -- but only if we work together to build a winning organization, starting right now.

Creating a powerful community of sustaining monthly donors is one of the best ways to ensure Democrats everywhere have what it takes to win this year. If people like you chip in what they can afford, Democrats will be unstoppable in November.

**[Become a monthly sustaining donor today, Derek. Any amount will make a difference.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522313/4067b0a/4aed063eb/7e5ea29a/1967342944/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dnc_monthly_sustainer?recurring=true&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+Become+a+monthly+sustaining+donor+todayC&utm_campaign=em_20180112_lc_nd&source=em_20180112_lc_nd&refcode=em_20180112_lc_nd")**

Your monthly donation will ensure we have the resources to take our winning strategy from Virginia and Alabama to every state in the country.

It will allow Democrats to invest in on-the-ground organizing to reach out to voters in every single community.

And it will help Democrats win up and down the ballot in November.

You can make a difference in this election, Derek. Will you step up today to show you stand with Democrats?

**[Become a monthly sustaining donor today to defeat Republicans and elect Democrats, from the school board to the Oval Office.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522313/4067b0a/4aed063eb/7e5ea298/1967342944/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dnc_monthly_sustainer?recurring=true&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=3+-+Become+a+monthly+sustaining+donor+today+&utm_campaign=em_20180112_lc_nd&source=em_20180112_lc_nd&refcode=em_20180112_lc_nd")**

Thanks,

Laura

name: Debbie Wasserman Schultz
subject: Stopping sexual harassment can start in Congress
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail
body:
| |
---
| |
---
| If you're a woman, you've probably heard the excuses before. _'She was dressed provocatively.' 'She was asking for it.' 'It's just locker room talk.'_ The recent outpouring of workplace sexual harassment allegations might come as a shock to some men among us -- and to everyone, these incidents are beyond troubling. But I'd be willing to guess most women in this country aren't too surprised. The unfortunate truth is that sexual harassment, especially in the workplace, has been a quietly accepted facet of our culture for a long time. The recent #metoo movement has shed some much-needed light on this problem, and I think it's far past time we bring that movement to the next level. I'm a big believer in leading by example. And I believe that if we want to root out institutionalized sexual harassment in the workplace, we should start in our own workplace. **I have a plan to lift the veil of secrecy hiding sexual harassment settlements against Members of Congress and staff, and to bring a culture of accountability to Capitol Hill.** Today, I'm asking you to lend your support to this effort by becoming a Citizen Co-Sponsor. [_**Find out more about the plan and sign on to show your support.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113fab/622b7b5b/18a6a7f7/307bbb46/1958902503/VEsH/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e180108") Regardless of the poor moral leadership shown by our president, it's time we take steps toward ending sexual harassment in America. Thank you for adding your support. Debbie |

Team,

We've had enough of Donald Trump's sexist name-calling.

Just this week he called Dianne Feinstein "sneaky Dianne."

But this isn't the first time Trump has resorted to name-calling our female Senators. We all know he's repeatedly called Elizabeth Warren "Pocahontas." And just last month, he called Kirsten Gillibrand a "lightweight" and attacked her with sexist innuendo.

Trump's sexist name-calling dismisses and demeans women. It's a blatant attempt to discourage women's leadership -- and we've had enough.

**We know the best way to fight back: re-elect our Democratic Senate women so that they can keep standing up to Trump, no matter how hard he attacks them.**

[**Dianne, Elizabeth, Kirsten and eight more Democratic women are up for re-election. Pitch in $1 to the Women 's Senate Network to re-elect all eleven of our Senate women now. **](http://action.dscc. But we know that as the election year begins, the attacks will only get worse, and our Democratic Senate women need our support now more than ever.

name: Christina Reynolds, EMILY's List
subject: We can't let these Senate women down
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6aea/24c69fd9/138eef664/18928a19/1531553804/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFEM19N_digital_email_20180112_Senate10_inact&source=WQEFEM19N_digital_email_20180112_Senate10_inact")
---

At first glance, the Senate map looks tough for Democrats this year.

While Republicans have to defend just eight seats, Democrats are defending _**24**_ , including 10 EMILY's List women.

But the last year has shown that it's Republicans who should be nervous -- from the millions of activists showing up to women's marches to the blue wave in Virginia to the Democratic victory in Alabama.

Defending so many seats won't be easy, but our Senate women aren't afraid of tough fights. They fight for us against the Trump agenda every day.

**[Donate $3 to help re-elect all 10 EMILY's List Senate women.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6aea/24c69fd9/138eef664/18928a1a/1531553804/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180112_email_Senate10?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+to+help+reelect+all+10+EMILYs+L&utm_campaign=WQEFEM19N_digital_email_20180112_Senate10_inact&source=WQEFEM19N_digital_email_20180112_Senate10_inact")**

Three of our senators are running in states Donald Trump won in 2016: Tammy Baldwin in Wisconsin, Claire McCaskill in Missouri, and Debbie Stabenow in Michigan.

Republicans have already spent $3.1 million on attack ads against Tammy. That's more than the GOP has spent against all the other Democratic Senate candidates combined.

And we have two women in Minnesota -- Amy Klobuchar and Tina Smith -- where Trump lost by a single point.

But this community is strong, and I know we value our Senate women too much to let them down.

**Donate $3 to fight for our Senate women as they've fought for us -- and so they can keep fighting for us:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Support-Our-Senators](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6aea/24c69fd9/138eef664/18928a1b/1531553804/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180112_email_Senate10?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgSupportOurSenato&utm_campaign=WQEFEM19N_digital_email_20180112_Senate10_inact&source=WQEFEM19N_digital_email_20180112_Senate10_inact")**

Thanks,

Christina Reynolds
Vice President of Communications, EMILY's List

name: Russ Feingold, LegitAction
subject: The census is under threat
body:
[ Legit ]LegitAction

Friend,

Every state in America relies on census data to design its electoral
maps. An attack on the legitimacy of the census is an attack on the
legitimacy of our democracy.

President Trump wants to nominate Thomas Brunell, a professor who
literally wrote the book on how to gerrymander, as deputy director of
the Census Bureau. Brunell at the helm of the Bureau could undermine
our democratic legitimacy for decades.

But there’s still time to intervene. And as a LegitAction member, I’m
calling on you to lead the charge on this latest danger to our voting
rights.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/sign/oppose_brunell_nomination/?t=1002&akid=540%2E356062%2EaI3Sdo ]Sign LegitAction’s petition: Oppose the appointment of partisan
redistricting advocate Thomas Brunell to serve as deputy director at
the U.S. Census Bureau.

Top Census Bureau officials have historically been career civil
servants with a background in managing federal agencies. Thomas
Brunell has zero public service to his name.

But it’s not just a lack of qualifications that makes Brunell so
dangerous -- it’s his anti-voter agenda. He helped red states like
North Carolina and Alabama draw heavily gerrymandered districts that
effectively silence progressives and voters of color. As deputy
director of the Census Bureau, he could skew the design of the census
itself, leading to partisan electoral maps nationwide.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/sign/oppose_brunell_nomination/?t=1003&akid=540%2E356062%2EaI3Sdo ]Thomas Brunell has no place at the Census Bureau. Oppose Brunell
for deputy director of the U.S. Census Bureau. Sign the LegitAction
petition today.

Whenever LegitAction members work together, we achieve meaningful
victories against those who seek to undermine our votes. I know we can
help stop this, too.

Thank you for standing up for democratic legitimacy today, and every
day.

Russ Feingold

clean_body: [ Legit ]LegitAction Friend, Every state in America relies on census data to design its electoral maps. An attack on the legitimacy of the census is an attack on the legitimacy of our democracy. President Trump wants to nominate Thomas Brunell, a professor who literally wrote the book on how to gerrymander, as deputy director of the Census Bureau. Brunell at the helm of the Bureau could undermine our democratic legitimacy for decades. But there’s still time to intervene. And as a LegitAction member, I’m calling on you to lead the charge on this latest danger to our voting rights. [ ]Sign LegitAction’s petition: Oppose the appointment of partisan redistricting advocate Thomas Brunell to serve as deputy director at the U.S. Census Bureau. Top Census Bureau officials have historically been career civil servants with a background in managing federal agencies. Thomas Brunell has zero public service to his name. But it’s not just a lack of qualifications that makes Brunell so dangerous -- it’s his anti-voter agenda. He helped red states like North Carolina and Alabama draw heavily gerrymandered districts that effectively silence progressives and voters of color. As deputy director of the Census Bureau, he could skew the design of the census itself, leading to partisan electoral maps nationwide. [ ]Thomas Brunell has no place at the Census Bureau. Oppose Brunell for deputy director of the U.S. Census Bureau. Sign the LegitAction petition today. Whenever LegitAction members work together, we achieve meaningful victories against those who seek to undermine our votes. I know we can help stop this, too. Thank you for standing up for democratic legitimacy today, and every day. Russ Feingold LegitAction [ ]TAKE ACTION » Authorized and paid for by LegitAction, Inc. name: Chris Van Hollen
subject: Trump's racist remarks
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
| __
Yesterday, President Trump again revealed his racist and xenophobic core in remarks about immigrants fleeing Haiti, El Salvador, and some African nations.

His disgusting comments (made in a meeting about the future of a program that protects people fleeing natural disasters, war, and political strife) violate our fundamental values and the essence of what it means to be an American.

For a long time we have known that Donald Trump is unfit to be president. But Trump's behavior, demonstrated almost daily in words and deeds, is actively undermining the respect and credibility of our country around the world.

[**We cannot afford to look away. It is long past time that our Republican colleagues put patriotism before politics and demand that President Trump be held accountable for his conduct.**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91864/70cdc2ba/46e8e725/39021b3/2932583714/VEsH/ "https://cvanhollen.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/hold-president-trump-accountable?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+We+cannot+afford+to+look+away+It+is+long&utm_campaign=CVH_EM_PET_FULL_180112&source=CVH_EM_PET_FULL_180112")

As we approach the birthday of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr., I am reminded of his statement, "The ultimate tragedy is not the oppression and cruelty by the bad people, but the silence over that by the good people."

I commend Senator Flake for his principled statements earlier today about Trump's racist remarks. For the good of our country, every member of Congress, regardless of party, should condemn the president's comments and demand an apology.

[**Please reach out to those you know around the country and ask them to call their members of Congress and demand that they end their silence and speak out.**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91864/70cdc2ba/46e8e725/39021b0/2932583714/VEsE/ "https://cvanhollen.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/hold-president-trump-accountable?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=2+-+Please+reach+out+to+those+you+know+aroun&utm_campaign=CVH_EM_PET_FULL_180112&source=CVH_EM_PET_FULL_180112")

Thank you.

Chris Van Hollen
U.S. Senator

**_A Note From Jeanie_**
**Dear Derek,**

**2017 was a great year for Republicans, filled with so many accomplishments. Proof positive that when NH Republicans lead, New Hampshire wins! **
****
**Last week we launched our new website[Accomplishments.GOP](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=Jmc2ycD1zgvqF-2FA6dObYe1Js-2BxVjlbwJx59enm7LesvAGXIFyU3YI0pyKovJhBQQ_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30MLtKTSwlbUxHp3SI0F9bn-2F-2FcqezpIm1OJ6SR8leImAR-2BVftbguHEUMdvErLekFQt1BHYTiWgvU37fDP2JQAw-2Bu1NHOf4ugMv5xY-2FtZQjoiKmHuDZzUXM3MuW3hNHy-2Fpn-2FbwPiWG-2Fq-2B8BgBsj0LJSReLgvED6Gdky2Sh6ysjeUV1EWDxfqEznqlBPXIBr2SBcF2v65BBotCTvADlkjgoI53IUxmNFnNZ2KCTI99cOvbroGYz19mCMi0hBk7WbkE3xTLtyi3alvLv9gb8FtscQL9A-3D-3D) to coincide with the one year anniversary of Governor Sununu’s inauguration. The website outlines the efforts that are moving the state forward. **
****
**And even though last week’s snowstorm delayed the one year anniversary cake presentation to Governor Sununu, it didn’t dampen the enthusiasm of those gathered round the Governor this week when he cut the cake and shared his optimism for 2018.**
****
**Some accomplishments include:**
****
* **Strengthening our election laws**
* **Sending $38 million back to our communities for road and bridge improvements**

* **Passing a fiscally conservative budget with no new taxes or fees**

* **Reducing regulations AND business taxes to spur our economy**

* **Doubling our investment in the Alcohol Fund so more resources are available to end the opioid crisis**
****

**To learn more about what has been accomplished in 2017 and to see what the legislative agenda is for 2018, check out[Accomplishments.GOP](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=Jmc2ycD1zgvqF-2FA6dObYe1Js-2BxVjlbwJx59enm7LesvAGXIFyU3YI0pyKovJhBQQ_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30MLtKTSwlbUxHp3SI0F9bn-2F-2FcqezpIm1OJ6SR8leImAR-2BVftbguHEUMdvErLekFQt1BHYTiWgvU37fDP2JQAw-2BuzrhQPd7V0CvKj9tiswf8wvEY8m8ASqyfrLKbampJTdZbYBySjRamPjezyOBUYLxgvkaRuiQYjkl-2BWicIYkwcN8ATGhUjYNM0-2BQ6Y2I-2FPlswUVp-2Bbqb5HVJmJ-2B1vQ-2F6Kp4SVZTL4rBar3HN3Mer-2BnA3sgTNVT3qnPOJSdOdSLVVA4XBHxSepyFeOub47YzJqww-3D-3D).**

We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6ae0/24c69fd9/1371b2747/1892be78/2964763218/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQENEM20N_digital_email_20180112_RisingStar1_inact&source=WQENEM20N_digital_email_20180112_RisingStar1_inact")

Peter,

Nominations for the annual Gabrielle Giffords Rising Star Award are now open!

This award is something we give out every year to a woman currently serving in state or local office who has a bright career in public service ahead of her.

Last year was huge for women in state and local politics, with so many of them setting up critical protections for their communities against the Trump agenda and so many new faces running and winning for the first time.

If you know an elected woman who is a leader in your community, we'd love to hear about her.

**[Who do you think should be the next Gabrielle Giffords Rising Star Award recipient? Nominate a pro-choice Democratic woman in state or local office who is doing great work.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6ae0/24c69fd9/1371b2747/1892be79/2964763218/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/s/20180112_email_RisingStar1?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Who+do+you+think+should+be+the+next+Gabr&utm_campaign=WQENEM20N_digital_email_20180112_RisingStar1_inact&source=WQENEM20N_digital_email_20180112_RisingStar1_inact")**

The Rising Star Award recognizes pro-choice Democratic women serving in state legislatures, mayors' offices, city councils, school boards, and beyond. It's a way to say thank you for her work and show that we will support her wherever her career takes her next.

Past winners include Philadelphia City Councilwoman Helen Gym, Colorado Speaker of the House Crisanta Duran, Boston City Councilwoman Ayanna Pressley, and former Georgia House Minority Leader and current gubernatorial candidate Stacey Abrams.

They all have something in common: They tirelessly fight for women, families, and their communities. And we know the 2018 Rising Star is out there doing the same thing right now.

But we need your help to find her and celebrate her work.

**Nominate a pro-choice Democratic woman serving in state or local office for the 2018 Gabrielle Giffords Rising Star Award:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Nominate-the-2018-Rising-Star](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6ae0/24c69fd9/1371b2747/1892be7a/2964763218/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/s/20180112_email_RisingStar1?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgNominatethe2018R&utm_campaign=WQENEM20N_digital_email_20180112_RisingStar1_inact&source=WQENEM20N_digital_email_20180112_RisingStar1_inact")**

Thanks,

Geri Prado
Senior Director of State & Local Campaigns, EMILY's List

name: Nina Turner, Our Revolution
subject: TPS for Haitian immigrants
body:
Trump's bigotry was on full display again yesterday. Meeting in the Oval Office
#
Friends,
President Donald Trump's bigotry was on full display again yesterday. Meeting in the Oval Office, he once again slandered the Haitian people, their country, and the people of African nations with no regard for the dignity, respect, and compassion that the office of president demands. Our Revolution rejects his claims, made out of personal ignorance and insecurity, and will oppose any effort to create division between the American people and our sisters and brothers in Haiti and countries across the world. There is no place in the political conversation for the words being reported on in the media.
[**It's up to all of us to challenge President Trump's slander. Add your name in support of extending Temporary Protected Status (TPS) to Haitian residents and the people of all other TPS-designated countries in the United States.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7ad8c/bed43eb/36f6658c/75fea07b/599273031/VEsH/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/extend-tps?source=em180112")
Eight years ago today an earthquake ripped through Haiti, leaving more than 300,000 people dead and over 1.5 million displaced in the most calamitous natural disaster ever to befall the island nation. It is an American value to welcome people fleeing strife, turmoil, and tragedy into our country, and that is what Temporary Protected Status does.
There are 46,000 Haitian people in the United States with TPS status--a fraction of the people who lost everything in the Haiti earthquake. In addition to Haitian residents in the United States on TPS, there are hundreds of thousands more from El Salvador, Honduras, Nicaragua, Somalia, Sudan, South Sudan, Nepal, Syria, and Yemen. Over the course of our nation's history, we have answered the call to be a refuge to those whose homes have been lost to natural disaster or human strife.
[**Join us in condemning President Trump's comments and supporting TPS for Haitian immigrants and those from all countries under TPS. We must challenge racism, bigotry, and prejudice with hope, inclusion, and love.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7ad8c/bed43eb/36f6658c/75fea07b/599273031/VEsE/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/extend-tps?source=em180112")
